Off Calendar Sep-29-2022 Continued To Oct-06-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Atty. Jesse L. Miller, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP (Tel.415.543.8700) for plaintiff;
Atty. Nancy Harris, Esq. of the Law Office of Nancy Harris (Tel. 510.356.2022) for defendant;
Atty. Susanne Kim, Esq., of the San Francisco Unified School District (Tel. 415.241.6054) General Counsel for defendant.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S Motion To Quash Subpoena Or Alternatively Motion For Protective Order Regarding Depositions Of Current And Former Board Of Education Commissioners And Former Superintendent.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge, counsel for plaintiff, and counsel for defendant (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter called, argument/discussions are held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court issued the following ruling: the motion is granted in part and denied in part. Protective order granted as to James Lam, Vincent Matthews, and Alison Collins.

The parties are to meet and confer regarding the proposed order. The order is to be in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and submitted to Judge Pro Tem Lynn at williamclynn@gmail.com.

A follow-up hearing is set for Thursday, October 6, 2022, at 9:00 a.m. on the Discovery Calendar. The Court notes that if the order is signed the matter may go off calendar.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Jane Hong Elsey, CSR #11975, Tel. 909.831.2644, Email:csrjane@hotmail.com. (302/JPT)
